title: Piebald - The ultimate agentic AI control experience for developers
rawContent: Piebald - The ultimate agentic AI control experience for developers Piebald Downloads Pricing Docs Roadmap Blog The ultimate agentic AI control experience for developers. Piebald puts you in the driver’s seat. Orchestrate dozens of parallel agents. Exercise full control over context. Build software agentically. Download What sets Piebald apart? Besides being fully cross-platform (no WSL or Git Bash required on Windows), Piebald stands out with dozens of unique features to give developers control over their agentic development. Read on. Use your own subscriptions. We support OpenAI*, Anthropic, and Google providers, as well as any compatible API. We’ll add integrated support for many additional popular services in the near future. You can also use your favorite AI subscriptions—including Claude Pro/Max , ChatGPT Pro/Plus , and Google AI Free/Pro/Ultra —directly inside Piebald. *Both completions and responses APIs are supported; see here . Upload in progress... Your video file is being uploaded. The currently loaded video is the source file. We've already logged in to Claude Code with our Claude Max subscription, so we can import our credentials directly into Piebald. Never lose a draft prompt. We’ve all had those agonizing moments when the 10-sentence prompt you’ve been working on is lost. You didn’t copy it, and there’s no way to retrieve it. No more. Piebald automatically saves all your prompts as you type them. Write, switch to another chat, get a new idea and write about it, and come back to your original prompt and continue where you left off. Upload in progress... Your video file is being uploaded. The currently loaded video is the source file. We send a message in one chat, then navigate to another with a partially written draft prompt preserved and waiting to be finished. Sessions are safely persisted. Ever had multiple agents running when suddenly your laptop froze or died? Resuming all those sessions is hopeless! In Piebald, all your sessions are accessible from the sidebar; session status is always preserved, so you can see which ones are working, which ones need your attention, and which ones are done. It may take a little getting used to, but even tool calls waiting for your approval will remain pending and intact across machine reboots. Upload in progress... Your video file is being uploaded. The currently loaded video is the source file. We start multiple chats in parallel, and one of them calls a tool that requires manual approval. We close the app and reopen it, and the pending tool call is still waiting and can be approved without difficulty. Agentic and fine-grained configuration. Agentic development shouldn’t lock you out of low-level inference controls. Fully customize the system prompt, inference-level hyperparameters like temperature, stop sequences, and max tokens, and provider-specific fields as well, even specify custom override fields as JSON! You can also enable/disable MCP servers and individual tools on a per-chat and per-profile basis. Upload in progress... Your video file is being uploaded. The currently loaded video is the source file. We open the chat's configuration, modify the system prompt to add a silly instruction, and disable all tools but two random ones. We then send a message and observe that the model recognizes only those two tools and follows the silly instruction. Profiles. Create reusable collections of configuration and tools, and use them for any conversation. You know how you might have to add “just chatting, don’t search my codebase” to your prompt to keep the model from scouring your codebase just to answer a technical question? Well, you could create a profile called “Chatting” with just web_fetch and web_search , or no tools at all. Upload in progress... Your video file is being uploaded. The currently loaded video is the source file. We create a new profile named “Chatting” with access to no tools, and then using that profile we send a message and explicitly ask the model to use tools. It then objects and correctly explains that it has no tools. Inspect raw network traffic. The agent loop is a series of HTTP requests. With Piebald Pro, you can understand how it all works under the hood—what every agentic tool is actually doing to get you the smooth experience you know. All the HTTP requests that power your agentic development experience are visible in the HTTP traffic inspector—requests, responses, bodies, headers, status codes, durations, even streamed SSE chunks with their intervals. MCP traffic inspection coming soon. Upload in progress... Your video file is being uploaded. The currently loaded video is the source file. We send a message and then watch its underlying HTTP request appear in the HTTP traffic viewer in real-time. (Saying “You are Claude Code, ...” is required for applications to use Claude Pro/Max.) Agent Skills, Plan Mode, Compaction. All the agentic capabilities you’re familiar with from other tools are available for free in Piebald. Load agent skills to augment the AI’s capabilities. Use plan mode to have the model devise solutions and await your approval. Manually compact your chats to preserve important context, but with a renewed context window, and configure a context usage threshold to automatically compact long conversations. Upload in progress... Your video file is being uploaded. The currently loaded video is the source file. We instruct the model to implement support for Crush in Splitrail , and Piebald picks up the agent skills. The model proposes a plan—which we're able to copy to the clipboard—and we finally compact the conversation. More coming. We're in active development. Expect more features weekly—and check out our roadmap. Piebald The ultimate agentic AI control experience for developers.
